programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: The mission is still imminent, but something pressing came up.. some really cool guns! This course of fire includes an amazing rifle, a man down and an opportunity to go full-auto. Then, Crossfire lets the team try out a tactical bat that includes a 22LR Pistol and a window breaker spike. If that’s not enough to get the blood pumping, the contestants try out a silencer fit for the most secretive of missions and a John Wick shotgun. Buckle up.

programme.title: Aftermath
programme.desc: From suppressors to a shotgun fresh off the movie set of John Wick 4, the contestants got spoiled this week with amazing tactical weapons. Mark Baylis, Green Beret, also sits down with our hosts to talk about his invaluable nonprofit that is changing the lives of hundreds of American Veterans.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: The night mission is a go.. or so they thought. As the team readied for the night mission, word from HQ derails the plan. The team has to act fast to adapt to an ever-changing hostage situation. With an ingenious plan formed, some contestants have to go undercover to get the intel needed to complete the mission. See who this season's winner is on the nail-biter season finale of Surviving Mann.

programme.title: Aftermath
programme.desc: With the mission imminent, Chris and Cindy sit down with Danny and Kyle for some last words on how the competition has been going so far. Cindy also chats with Autumn from Stream2Sea about her revolutionary product the team used today to beat the Florida sun.

This short film is the official preview trailer for our full 2-hour cinematic nature experience, Europe’s Natural Wonders – Episode 2: Croatia’s Plitvice Lakes, now streaming in breathtaking 4K on NatureStream.tv. 🎬 Journey along elevated wooden boardwalks as crystal waters flow from one lake to another through lush karst landscapes. programme.title: World Poker Tour
programme.desc: The World Poker Tour returns to Canada for the $5,000 buy-in WPT Fallsview Poker Classic. The event attracted a field of 419 entrants, creating a prize pool of more than $1.5 million, and the overwhelming chip leader at the final table is Season XII WPT champion Anthony Zinno.

programme.title: World Poker Tour
programme.desc: Season XII WPT champion Anthony Zinno remains the chip leader of the $5,000 buy-in WPT Fallsview Poker Classic. Zinno battles Corey Hochman, David Cloutier, and Mark Bailey for the lion’s share of the more than $1.5 million prize pool.

programme.title: World Poker Tour
programme.desc: Only three players remain in the Season XIII WPT Fallsview Poker Classic. For the first time, Season XII WPT champion Anthony Zinno is on the short stack. Zinno, Corey Hochman, and Mark Bailey battle for the more than $300,000 first-place prize, a Hublot® Oceanographic 4000 Watch, and a pair of Monster® 24K Headphones.

programme.title: World Poker Tour
programme.desc: Fresh off of his win in the WPT Fallsview Poker Classic, Anthony Zinno goes for back-to-back victories and his third World Poker Tour title at the WPT L.A. Poker Classic. He competes against Chris Klodnicki, Igor Yaroshevsky, Mike Leah, Peter Neff, and Peter Tran for the more than $1 million top prize.

programme.title: World Poker Tour
programme.desc: With more than $1 million up for grabs, Anthony Zinno remains in the hunt for back-to-back victories and his third World Poker Tour title at the WPT L.A. Poker Classic. Standing in his way are seasoned professionals Mike Leah and Chris Klodnicki, along with Ukranian Igor Yaroshevsky and local Peter Neff.

programme.title: Twitch Celebrity Cash Game - Part 2 - Rampage - Poker Night In America

programme.title: Twitch Celebrity Cash Game - Part 3 - The Kissing Bet - Poker Night In America

programme.title: Around the ACL
programme.desc:
ATA 224: Mesa Signature Preview: The Final Stop Before Worlds
The final Signature event of the season has arrived, and this week on Around The ACL, Jake, Meesh, and Anthony break down every Pro Singles and Pro Doubles bracket heading into Mesa. With the World Championships just around the corner, every match matters as players look to build momentum, improve their seeding, and make one last statement before the biggest tournament of the year.
The crew analyzes all four Pro Singles brackets, highlighting favorable paths, dangerous sleepers, potential upsets, and the toughest roads to Championship Sunday. Then they turn their attention to Pro Doubles, debating which teams have the best chance to capture the final Signature title, which matchups could define the weekend, and who may be poised for a breakthrough performance.
Don't miss the final bracket breakdown before Worlds as the race for one last Signature championship takes center stage in Mesa.
